verdict_reasoning: EREG is an EGF-family ligand synthesized as a single-pass type I transmembrane proepiregulin. The membrane-anchored proform transits the plasma membrane and is cleaved by ADAM17 to release the soluble active ligand that signals through EGFR/ERBB4. The TM precursor stage is real and supports juxtacrine signaling, but it is transient and the shed soluble form is the dominant biological actor — fits dual_localization for shed-ligand-dominant TM proligands. Not secreted_only because a membrane-anchored stage exists; not yes/classical_surface_receptor because surface residency is brief.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether the membrane-anchored proEREG pool is stable enough on the surface to justify yes vs contextual.
